title: Say goodbye to busywork: Rethinking marketing workflows for a smarter future
summary: At the March MarTech Conference, we talked about transforming your workflow from a bottleneck into a growth engine. The post Say goodbye to busywork: Rethinking marketing workflows for a smarter future appeared first on MarTech .

title: Why emotion data is changing how ads get tested
summary: Emotion data reveals how audiences actually respond to creative, helping brands fix weak messaging before spending millions on media. The post Why emotion data is changing how ads get tested appeared first on MarTech .

title: Why Dove turned Reddit product feedback into a real-world campaign
summary: Emily Barfoot, head of Dove U.S., discusses how the campaign came together, navigating Reddit and the brand’s larger social media strategy.

title: Coors Banquet returns to live sports marketing following growth spurt
summary: The brand’s media budget is getting a double digit increase, with TV ads scheduled to air during the 2026 MLB and college football seasons.

title: Future of TV Briefing: OpenX adds attention targeting for CTV ads backed by TVision
summary: This week’s Future of TV Briefing looks at a new ad targeting option for CTV advertisers wary of people staring at their phones while an ad airs.

title: Marriott Bonvoy’s March Madness ads show key role hotels play in pregame rituals
summary: Along with ads spotlighting partnerships with the NCAA and U.S. Soccer Federation, the chain is launching a limited podcast tied to March Madness.

title: From rented audiences to engaged communities: Why participation is the new moat for publishers
summary: Mark Zohar, President and CEO, Viafoura For more than a decade, digital publishers have had to make a trade-off regarding social media. Social platforms promised reach, scale and frictionless distribution. In exchange, publishers ceded control of audience relationships, data...

title: Media Briefing: In the AI era, subscribers are the real prize — and the Telegraph proves it
summary: In an era where AI is eroding referral traffic and third-party distribution, a subscriber who pays directly has become the most valuable reader a publisher can own. Springer just bought over a million of them.

title: TreSemmé links with ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ for affordable luxury push
summary: The Unilever brand is focusing on traditional media for a campaign that features podcaster Paige DeSorbo and promotes its A-List Collection.
